title: dactylorhiza sphagnicola f. calcifugiens
intro: Dactylorhiza sphagnicola f. calcifugiens is a rare and endemic subspecies of orchid, specifically a variant of the sphagnum bog orchid, found in a limited region of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza sphagnicola f. calcifugiens are believed to include treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and fighting off infections due to its antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from the rhizome or roots, which can be dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and the need for further research.
